description: Behavioral test registry for Brite plugin skills (BC-2462)

test_cases.prompt: I need to redesign the auth system. It currently spans the API routes, middleware, and database layer — three separate modules. There are at least two viable approaches: JWT-based stateless auth or session-based with Redis. What should we do?
test_cases.notes: Core positive case — multi-module + multiple approaches should trigger brainstorming

test_cases.description: Brainstorming does NOT activate for trivial single-file change
test_cases.prompt: Rename the title in README.md from 'Project' to 'Brite Plugin Marketplace'.
test_cases.notes: Core negative case — trivial change should NOT trigger any skill

test_cases.description: Systematic debugging activates on bug report
test_cases.prompt: The login form submits successfully but the session cookie isn't being set. Users get logged out immediately after login. The auth endpoint returns 200 but the Set-Cookie header is missing from the response.
test_cases.notes: Bug report with symptoms — should trigger 4-phase debugging

test_cases.description: frontend-design activates for build/create UI task
test_cases.prompt: Build a login form component with email and password fields using React and Tailwind CSS. Include validation and a submit button.
test_cases.notes: Build/create keyword should route to frontend-design, NOT ui-ux-pro-max

test_cases.description: ui-ux-pro-max activates for design planning task
test_cases.prompt: Choose a color palette and font pairing for our SaaS analytics dashboard. We want a professional, data-heavy look that works well in dark mode.
test_cases.notes: Design planning (palette, fonts) should route to ui-ux-pro-max, NOT frontend-design

test_cases.prompt: Review the current UI for accessibility compliance. Audit the existing components against WCAG 2.1 guidelines and check for keyboard navigation issues.
test_cases.notes: Review/audit keywords should route to web-design-guidelines, NOT frontend-design

test_cases.description: Precedence: debugging beats brainstorming when bug is present
test_cases.prompt: I want to explore alternatives for the payment service architecture, but there's also a production bug — Stripe webhook events are being dropped and customers aren't getting order confirmations. Error logs show a 429 rate limit from Stripe.
test_cases.notes: When both debugging and brainstorming could apply, debugging should win (production bug takes priority)

test_cases.prompt: Set up ESLint and Prettier for this TypeScript monorepo. We need consistent formatting across all packages and strict type checking rules.
test_cases.notes: ESLint/Prettier setup should trigger code-quality skill

test_cases.description: No skill fires for irrelevant general-knowledge prompt
test_cases.prompt: What is the capital of France?
test_cases.notes: General knowledge question — no skill should activate, just answer directly

test_cases.description: Output quality: brainstorming produces thorough design exploration
test_cases.prompt: We need to add real-time notifications to the app. It touches the API layer (new WebSocket endpoints), the data layer (notification storage and read/unread state), and the React frontend (notification bell, dropdown, real-time updates). There are multiple approaches: WebSockets, Server-Sent Events, or polling.
test_cases.notes: Quality test — brainstorming output should be high-clarity, explore alternatives thoroughly
